question: Which university did the author of The Hobbit teach at?

context.title: J. R. R. Tolkien
context.text: J. R. R. Tolkien wrote The Hobbit and was a professor at Oxford University.

context.title: Oxford University
context.text: Oxford University is a collegiate research university in Oxford, England.

question: What instrument did the composer of The Four Seasons mainly play?

context.title: The Four Seasons
context.text: The Four Seasons is a group of violin concertos by Antonio Vivaldi.

context.title: Antonio Vivaldi
context.text: Antonio Vivaldi was an Italian Baroque composer and virtuoso violinist.

question: What field did the scientist who proposed the three laws of motion also study?

context.title: Isaac Newton
context.text: Isaac Newton formulated the three laws of motion and was a mathematician.

context.text: The three laws of motion are attributed to Isaac Newton.

question: In which country is the Eiffel Tower located?

context.title: Eiffel Tower
context.text: The Eiffel Tower is a wrought-iron lattice tower in Paris.

context.title: Paris
context.text: Paris is the capital city of France.

question: What ocean does the Amazon River empty into?

context.title: Amazon River
context.text: The Amazon River empties into the Atlantic Ocean near its delta.

context.title: Atlantic Ocean
context.text: The Atlantic Ocean is the second-largest ocean on Earth.

question: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?

context.title: Mars
context.text: Mars is known as the Red Planet due to its reddish appearance.

context.title: Solar System
context.text: Mars is the fourth planet from the Sun in the Solar System.

context.title: Mona Lisa
context.text: The Mona Lisa was painted by Leonardo da Vinci.

context.text: Leonardo da Vinci was an Italian Renaissance artist.

question: What element has the chemical symbol Au?

context.title: Au
context.text: The chemical symbol Au stands for gold.

context.title: Gold
context.text: Gold is a precious metal used in jewelry and electronics.

question: What is the boiling point of water in Celsius?

context.title: Water
context.text: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level.

context.title: Celsius scale
context.text: The Celsius scale sets boiling point of water at 100 degrees.

question: What is the chemical formula for water?

context.title: Water
context.text: Water has the chemical formula H2O consisting of hydrogen and oxygen.

context.title: H2O
context.text: H2O means two hydrogen atoms bonded to one oxygen atom.
